summary: On March 14, 2026, Ukrainian forces struck the Afipsky oil refinery in Krasnodar Krai with kamikaze drones, triggering a major fire confirmed by NASA FIRMS. The SIGNUM battalion destroyed a Russian tank camouflaged with netting using an FPV drone, while Russian forces countered with the "Elka" anti-drone system, which reportedly downed Ukrainian "Hornet" fixed-wing UAVs. In a separate incident, Ukrainian FPV drones eliminated Russian infantry who refused surrender after being offered terms by ground troops. Russian forces also destroyed Ukrainian unmanned boats in the Dnieper River delta using FPV drones. Ukrainian FPV units demonstrated tactical innovation, including targeting mounted troops and using dual strikes against concealed infantry, while Russian forces reported Ukrainian drones reaching 100 km behind lines with technology they claim to be unable to replicate.
